title: IV PSU+Scope Acquisition Config
description: Template for POST /api/iv/psu-scope/start. The PSU ramps voltage at psu_ramp_rate_v_s; the scope samples the shunt voltage on scope_channel at sample_rate_hz. Current is recovered as I = V_shunt / shunt_ohms (e.g. 1 mOhm shunt -> 4 mV/A). Every PSU OUTP/VOLT/CURR call routes through the basic-check gate (G1 #52). DEMO_MODE returns a synthetic single-diode sweep; in tests the PSU OUTPUT stays OFF.

shunt_ohms.description: Shunt resistance. Lab default 0.001 (1 mOhm) -> 4 mV per Amp.

scope_channel.description: DSO channel reading the shunt voltage (Keysight/Rigol DSO).

scope_timebase_ms.description: Horizontal timebase used during the sweep.

scope_trigger_v.description: Edge-trigger level on the shunt channel.

sample_rate_hz.description: Rate at which V/I pairs are pushed over the WebSocket.

sweeps.description: Number of full sweeps to record before the stream closes.

v_max.description: Sweep ceiling (typical Voc of one module string).

safety_notes: Every PSU OUTP/VOLT/CURR call is wrapped by _enforce_basic_check (G1 #52).
This module never enables PSU OUTPUT; the operator must do so explicitly after the Basic Check gate passes.
On WS disconnect the run helper drives V -> 0 and OUTP OFF best-effort.
DEMO_MODE bypasses all SCPI traffic and returns a synthetic single-diode sweep.
